---
title: "Mangrove of Surabaya"  
description: "The second story of my Internship at Environmental Agency of Surabaya (DLH Surabaya)."  
author: "Muhammad Zamharir"  
date: "2024-07-10"  
categories: [Research]  
image: "mangrove.jpg"  
---

## Overview

During my internship at the Surabaya Environmental Agency (DLH Surabaya), my colleagues and I, who are majoring in biology, were assigned a special project to assess the mangrove carbon stock in the eastern coastal area (Pamurbaya) and northern coastal area (Panturbaya) of Surabaya. In collaboration with DLH Surabaya staff, we conducted field studies across 17 sites within these two regions. The primary objective of this project was to estimate the carbon stock capacity of the mangrove ecosystems in Panturbaya and Pamurbaya, thereby providing DLH Surabaya with essential data to support local climate change mitigation policies and mangrove conservation initiatives.

## Findings🤓

For this study, we employed standardized methods for carbon stock estimation, including transect-quadrat sampling, species identification, diameter at breast height (DBH) measurements, and the application of allometric equations for biomass calculation. In addition, soil samples were collected and analyzed to determine Total Organic Carbon (TOC) content. The key findings of this research are summarized as follows:

-   Vegetation diversity: Dominant species include *Avicennia marina*, *Avicennia alba*, *Rhizophora stylosa*, *Sonneratia alba*, *Xylocarpus moluccensis*, and *Excoecaria agallocha*.
-   Carbon stock results of Pamurbaya is bigger (\~8,064 tons/ha) compared to Panturbaya (\~5,720 tons/ha).
-   Condition assessment: Most sites classified as “good–very dense” (\>1500 trees/ha). However, some sites (e.g., Kenjeran) show moderate damage due to pollution, land conversion, and industrial activity.
-   Mangroves in Surabaya store significantly more carbon than many other forest types, making them critical for climate resilience.

## Records🖼️

![Figure 1. Diameter at breast height (DBH) measurements](IMG-20240605-WA0120.jpg){fig-align="center"}

![Figure 2. Species identification and documentation](IMG-20240605-WA0093.jpg){fig-align="center"}

![Figure 3. Sunbathing at 2 PM](IMG-20240605-WA0101.jpg)

![Figure 4. The aftermath (of one site 🤡🤡)](IMG-20240605-WA0131.jpg){fig-align="center"}

![Figure 5. The Final Report](mangrove%20report.png){fig-align="center"}
